init.title: Learn React and Next.js with Hooks: Practical Exercise Guide
init.description: Hooks are functions that let you “hook into” React's state and lifecycle from function components. Before them, only classes could manage state or effects; now, with hooks like useState, useEffect, useContext, and many more, function components can do the exact same thing in a simpler, more reusable way.

hooks.title: What are Hooks?
description: Hooks are functions that let you “hook into” React's state and lifecycle from function components.

Before them, only classes could manage state or effects; now, with hooks like useState, useEffect, useContext, and many more, function components can do the exact same thing in a simpler, more reusable way.

levels.title: How are the exercises organized?
levels.description: Each hook is presented with 3 difficulty levels:

level.description: To master the syntax and basic concepts.
More realistic problems that combine logic and dependencies.
Exercises inspired by real applications, production-ready.

rules.title: Rules of Hooks (important)
rules.description: Always remember these two rules when working with hooks:
rule: Only call them at the top level of your component or custom hook. Don't put them inside conditionals, loops, or nested functions.
Only call them from React functions: function components or your own custom hooks.

start.title: Ready to get started?
description: Pick a hook, solve the exercises, and experiment by modifying the code. The best way to learn is by writing, breaking, and fixing things.
Once you feel comfortable, try combining several hooks in the same component… that's how real applications are built!
